Code quality metric #384: Measure cyclomatic complexity to assess code maintainability.
Cyclomatic complexity measures the number of independent paths through a code module, helping identify code that is difficult to maintain and test.
explanation: Cyclomatic complexity is calculated by counting decision points (if, while, for, catch statements) in code. A complexity of 1-5 is considered simple and maintainable, 5-10 is moderate, and above 10 requires refactoring. Tools like SonarQube help measure and monitor this metric automatically.
